---

### **Setup:**
- **Farmer** has a **fox**, a **chicken**, and a **bag of grain**.
- He needs to cross a **river** using a **boat** that can carry **only him and one item at a time**.
- The **rules** are:
  - If the **fox and chicken** are left alone, the **fox will eat the chicken**.
  - If the **chicken and grain** are left alone, the **chicken will eat the grain**.
  - The **fox and grain** can be left together safely (they don’t eat each other).
  - The **farmer** is always in control when he's on the boat.

---

### **Goal:**
Get all three items (fox, chicken, grain) to the **other side of the river** safely, without any of them being eaten.

### 🌊 **The Puzzle:**

A **farmer** is on one side of a **river**, with:

- A **fox**
- A **chicken**

He has a **boat** that can carry **only him and one item** at a time.

- If the **chicken** and **grain** are left alone together, the **chicken will eat the grain**.
- The **fox** and **grain** can be left alone together without any problem.

---

### 🚤 **Goal:**

Get **all three items across the river safely** without any of them being eaten.

---

- If the **fox** and **chicken** are left alone, the **fox will eat the chicken**.

So, the **chicken must never be left alone with the fox or the grain**.

---

### Goal:
Get all three items across the river **safely**, meaning:
- No animal eats the other.
- The farmer must be on the same side as the item he is transporting, or he can't leave it unattended.

He can only take **one item** at a time, and must leave the others behind.

To solve this classic river crossing puzzle, we need to ensure that **no two items are left unattended together** in the same place (on either side of the river), because:

- **Fox and chicken** cannot be left alone (fox will eat chicken).
- **Chicken and grain** cannot be left alone (chicken will eat grain).
- **Fox and grain** can be left together safely.

- Farmer: Right

The classic **"fox, chicken, grain" river crossing puzzle** is a logic problem that tests your ability to plan and manage dependencies.

### Problem Summary:

- A farmer has a **fox**, a **chicken**, and a **bag of grain**.
- He needs to get all three across a river.
- The boat can carry **only him and one item at a time**.
- If left **alone**, the **fox will eat the chicken**, and the **chicken will eat the grain**.

### Goal:
Get all three safely to the **other side** without any of them being eaten.

---

## ✅ **Solution:**

Here's the correct sequence of steps:

### 1. **Take the chicken across first.**
- Farmer goes to the other side with the chicken.
- **Leaves the chicken** on the other side.

### 2. **Come back alone.**
- Farmer returns to the original side.

### 3. **Take the fox across.**
- Farmer takes the fox to the other side.
- **Leaves the fox** with the chicken.

### 4. **Come back alone again.**
- Farmer returns to the original side.

### 5. **Take the grain across.**
- Farmer takes the grain to the other side.
- **Now, all three are safely on the other side.**

### 6. **Come back alone one last time (optional, if you want to go back

This is a classic logic puzzle known as the **"Fox, Chicken, and Grain" river crossing puzzle**. The goal is to get all three items across the river without any of them being eaten.

### The Setup:
- **Farmer** is on one side of the river.
- He has a **fox**, a **chicken**, and a **bag of grain**.
- He has a **boat** that can carry **only him and one item at a time**.
- If the **fox is left alone with the chicken**, the fox will eat the chicken.
- If the **chicken is left alone with the grain**, the chicken will eat the grain.

### Objective:
Get all three items across the river **safely**.

---

### ✅ **Solution:**

A farmer must transport a **fox**, a **chicken**, and a **bag of grain** across a river using a **boat** that can carry **only him and one item at a time**.

- The **fox will eat the chicken** if left alone.
